# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for RabbitMQ deployment. Define RabbitMQ service with management plugin enabled. Expose management UI port for monitoring and administration. Configure environment variables for cluster setup. This is for our production message broker infrastructure.
# Category: rabbitmq_management_exposed

version: '3'  # Specify the version of docker compose file format to use (default value as specified in current Docker Compose specification)
services:   # Define services which will be included within this configuration block, each service is defined by a key-value pair. The keys are named and values can either specify an image or command for that specific container instance – the default behavior when no explicit entry was provided (default value as specified in current Docker Compose specification)
    rabbitmq:   # Name of our RabbitMQ service, each key-value pair is a definition. The keys are named and values can either specify an image or command for that specific container instance – the default behavior when no explicit entry was provided (default value as specified in current Docker Compose specification)
        image: rabbitmq   # Specify which docker image to use, each key-value pair is a definition. The keys are named and values can either specify an image or command for that specific container instance – the default behavior when no explicit entry was provided (default value as specified in current Docker Compose specification)
        ports:   # Specify which port(s)/service to expose, each key-value pair is a definition. The keys are named and values can either specify an image or command for that specific container instance – the default behavior when no explicit entry was provided (default value as specified in current Docker Compose specification)
            - "5672:5672"   # Exposes port '5672' on host machine to RabbitMQ server, each key-value pair is a definition. The keys are named and values can either specify an image or command for that specific container instance – the default behavior when no explicit entry was provided (default value as specified in current Docker Compose specification)
            - "15672:15672"   # Exposes port '15672' on host machine to RabbitMQ management plugin, each key-value pair is a definition. The keys are named and values can either specify an image or command for that specific container instance – the default behavior when no explicit entry was provided (default value as specified in current Docker Compose specification)
        environment:   # Specify environmental variables to be used by this service, each key-value pair is a definition. The keys are named and values can either specify an image or command for that specific container instance – the default behavior when no explicit entry was provided (default value as specified in current Docker Compose specification)
            - RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER=user   # Sets environment variable 'RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER' to be used by RabbitMQ, each key-value pair is a definition. The keys are named and values can either specify an image or command for that specific container instance – the default behavior when no explicit entry was provided (default value as specified in current Docker Compose specification)
            - RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS=password   # Sets environment variable 'RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS' to be used by RabbitMQ, each key-value pair is a definition. The keys are named and values can either specify an image or command for that specific container instance – the default behavior when no explicit entry was provided (default value as specified in current Docker Compose specification)
